com.mentor.chs.api.icd

Enum XICDTagType

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.lang.Enum<XICDTagType>
      • com.mentor.chs.api.icd.XICDTagType
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.io.Serializable, java.lang.Comparable<XICDTagType>

    @NotToBeImplemented
public enum XICDTagType
extends java.lang.Enum<XICDTagType>

    Enum which will used to define the type of an ICD applicability tag, See IXICDTag. New applicability tag types may be added to this enum in the future.

    Tags of an ICD are processed to determine if an ICD is applicable to a design. The input to this process is
    • The collection of tags of the ICD
    • The design for which the ICD applicability has to be determined
    • Optionally, a buildlist.
    The system determines if the ICD is applicable to the specified design in the following way:
    • The names of the tags of the type APPLICABILITY_PROJECT form the collection of the applicable project names.
    • The names tags of the type APPLICABILITY_DESIGN provide a collection of applicable design names.

    The ICD is applicable to the specified design only if all of the following conditions are true

    1. The collection of applicable projects is empty or contains the name of the project of the specified design
    2. The collection of applicable design names is empty or contains the name of the specified design
    3. No buildlist has been specified as input OR The design is contained in the input buildlist and the input tags contain a tag of type APPLICABILITY_BUILDLIST whose name is same as the name of the input buildlist.
    In a design application, to determine if an ICD is applicable to a design that is opened, the ICD tags are processed with the the 'Active' buildlist as the input buildlist.
    See Also:
    IXICDTag

    • Enum Constant Summary

      Enum Constants 
      Enum Constant and Description
      APPLICABILITY_BUILDLIST
      The name of a tag of this type refers to a buildlist name.
      APPLICABILITY_DESIGN
      The name of a tag of this type refers to a design name.
      APPLICABILITY_PROJECT
      The name of a tag of this type refers to a project name.

      All Methods Static Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      static XICDTagType valueOf(java.lang.String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this type with the specified name.
      static XICDTagType[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared.

    • Enum Constant Detail

      • APPLICABILITY_PROJECT

        public static final XICDTagType APPLICABILITY_PROJECT
        The name of a tag of this type refers to a project name. Multiple tags of this type can be present on an ICD.

      • APPLICABILITY_BUILDLIST

        public static final XICDTagType APPLICABILITY_BUILDLIST
        The name of a tag of this type refers to a buildlist name. Multiple tags of this type can be present on an ICD.

      • APPLICABILITY_DESIGN

        public static final XICDTagType APPLICABILITY_DESIGN
        The name of a tag of this type refers to a design name. Multiple tags of this type can be present on an ICD

    • Method Detail

      • values

        public static XICDTagType[] values()
        Returns an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared. This method may be used to iterate over the constants as follows: 
        for (XICDTagType c : XICDTagType.values())
    System.out.println(c);
        Returns:
        an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared

      • valueOf

        public static XICDTagType valueOf(java.lang.String name)
        Returns the enum constant of this type with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this type. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
        Parameters:
        name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
        Returns:
        the enum constant with the specified name
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if this enum type has no constant with the specified name
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if the argument is null
